description: GS1900-8HP A1 (formerly v2) 8-port PoE+ managed gigabit switch, Realtek RTL8380M, 70W PoE budget

hardware.serial: UART header, 115200 8N1, 3.3V (left side of PCB, labeled VCC/TX/RX/GND)
hardware.note: Dual-partition OEM device. Shares casing/platform with GS1900-10HP. PoE MCU initialized by bootloader at power-on (all ports PoE+ enabled by default). MCU state lost on reboot. CRITICAL: Serial numbers starting S21+ must run stock v2.70+ first or PoE can be irreversibly bricked.

oem-http.description: Upload initramfs image via OEM web UI HTTP upload endpoint using curl. Two-stage: OEM upload boots initramfs, then sysupgrade writes permanently. Same ZyXEL firmware stack as GS1900-24E.

sysupgrade.description: SSH sysupgrade from existing OpenWrt (initramfs or permanent OpenWrt). Works for same-version or compatible version upgrades.

mtd-write.description: Direct MTD partition write from running OpenWrt initramfs. Used when sysupgrade rejects images due to partition layout changes between versions.

serial-tftp-openwrt.description: Flash via serial + TFTP. Requires FTDI adapter (3.3V) connected to UART header on left side of PCB. Two-stage: boot initramfs via TFTP, then sysupgrade.
serial-tftp-openwrt.reset_instructions: Connect FTDI serial adapter to UART header (3.3V, left side PCB). Interrupt U-Boot with space bar within 1 second at boot.

uboot_env.note: bootdelay=1 set by OEM. Dual-partition device — use 'setsys bootpartition 0' and 'savesys' before TFTP.

partition_layout.note: Two layouts exist depending on firmware. OEM uses dual-partition. OpenWrt 25.12.1 sysupgrade merges to single partition.

openwrt_25_merged.description: Merged single-partition layout after OpenWrt 25.12.1 sysupgrade

oem_login.source: Verified on live hardware (S/N XXXXX-XXXXXXXXX, Rev A1), 2026-05-21
oem_login.password_change_note: V2.80+ forces password change from default 1234 on first login

oem_firmware_page.source: Verified on live hardware, 2026-05-21 (both V2.00 and V2.90)
oem_firmware_page.confirm_dialog: Do you really want to reboot?
oem_firmware_page.flash_message: Writing image to FLASH... Do not power down...

openwrt_booting.note: Initramfs boots fast (RAM-only, no flash write). Permanent install takes longer due to JFFS2 overlay init (~30s) during first boot.

openwrt_ready.note: No LuCI web UI in default build (switch device, no wifi). SSH only. SCP must use -O flag (no sftp-server in initramfs). Root login without password by default.

port_config_template.note: PoE port IDs match interface numbers: lan1=1, lan2=2, ..., lan8=8

poe.note: Same PoE behavior as B1 revision. Bootloader enables PoE+ on all ports. realtek-poe package included by default.

safety.serial_number_warning: Devices with serial numbers starting S21 or higher MUST run stock firmware v2.70+ before any firmware changes. Older stock firmware can irreversibly brick PoE capabilities.
safety.dual_partition_note: Dual-partition device. Keep OEM on backup partition for recovery.

oem-stock-upgrade.verified_with: V2.00(AAHI.2) → V2.90(AAHI.0) via Playwright HTTP upload, cmd=5903, partition=0 (Active)
oem-stock-upgrade.notes: Firmware upgrade via OEM web UI. 5.8MB .bix file uploaded via httpupload.cgi. Flash write + reboot took ~90s. V2.90 forces mandatory password change from default 1234. Hardware revision A1 confirmed by v2.90 dashboard. New credentials: admin/Zyxel2026!.

oem-http.verified_with: Uploaded OpenWrt 25.12.1 initramfs via OEM HTTP to V2.90 OEM firmware, device booted into initramfs in ~24s
oem-http.notes: Initramfs upload via same cmd=5903/httpupload.cgi endpoint as stock firmware update. Filename must be ≤64 chars (v2.90 limit). OpenWrt default filename (75 chars) rejected — renamed to openwrt-initramfs.bin. Login with admin/Zyxel2026! (changed from default by v2.90 mandatory password change). Initramfs booted in ~24s (very fast, RAM-only).

sysupgrade.verified_with: sysupgrade -n from 25.12.1 initramfs to 25.12.1 squashfs permanent install, SSH access after ~105s boot
sysupgrade.notes: SCP requires -O flag (legacy protocol) — OpenWrt initramfs lacks sftp-server. sysupgrade completed successfully, ~105s boot time. MTD layout merged from dual 6976K+6976K partitions to single 13824K firmware partition. JFFS2 overlay init takes ~30s during first boot. All 8 PoE ports enabled by default in /etc/config/poe.

serial-tftp-openwrt.notes: UNTESTED. U-Boot env verified (bootdelay=1, boardmodel=ZyXEL_GS1900_8HP). serverip=192.168.1.X (should update to 192.168.1.2). Serial commands documented from OpenWrt source.

ssh.description: Restricted Cisco-like CLI via /bin/cli binary (426KB, symlinked to /bin/login). NOT a Linux shell.
not_available: No filesystem access (ls, cd, cat, cp)
ssh.verdict: No code execution possible via SSH

diagnostic_cli.cve: CVE-2019-15803 (hidden functionality), CVE-2019-15801 (hardcoded passwords), CVE-2019-15802 (hardcoded AES key)
diagnostic_cli.verdict: Register-level hardware access only. No process execution, no filesystem access, no shell.
diagnostic_cli.note: PoE control on stock firmware requires web UI or HTTP API. The diagnostic 'port' command is for PHY registers, not PoE management.

poe_control.description: PoE port enable/disable via HTTP form POST to dispatcher.cgi
poe_control.note: Third-party tools like zyxel-poe-manager automate this. conwrt has oem_http_login and zyxel_encode_password in scripts/flash/oem_handlers.py.

system_cmd_injection.description: system() call in libclicmd.so with insufficient input sanitization
system_cmd_injection.status: Unexplored. Could theoretically grant shell access but high risk and may crash management daemon.

arbitrary_code_execution.http_api: Not possible via normal API. CVE-2019-15800 is theoretical vector.
arbitrary_code_execution.serial_uboot: Possible - can set init=/bin/sh bootargs, but requires serial adapter
arbitrary_code_execution.firmware_upload_to_initramfs: Possible - upload OpenWrt initramfs via httpupload.cgi, device boots full Linux from RAM

from_openwrt.description: Switch running OpenWrt permanently can serve as flash host for target devices
from_openwrt.poe_control: ubus call poe manage or uci set poe.@port[N].enable

from_stock_via_initramfs.description: Boot OpenWrt initramfs from RAM on stock V2.90 switch, then use it as self-contained flash host. Stock flash is never modified.
steps: 1. Upload OpenWrt initramfs image via stock V2.90 web UI (cmd=5903, httpupload.cgi)
2. Switch reboots into OpenWrt running entirely from RAM (~24s boot)
3. Upload conwrt-lite + firmware image via SCP to /tmp
4. Run conwrt-lite: PoE control, TFTP serve, SSH verify — all from the switch
5. Reboot switch → returns to stock V2.90, untouched
advantages: Self-contained — no external host needed during flash
Non-destructive — stock firmware on flash is preserved
Full Linux environment — busybox, dropbear, realtek-poe, tftp
128MB RAM is plenty for initramfs + tools + firmware image
requirements: conwrt-lite scripts (POSIX shell, no Python needed)
Initial web UI access to upload initramfs (one-time per session)
limitations: PoE power lost during switch reboot (MCU reinit) — target device power-cycles with the switch
Must re-upload initramfs via web UI after each power cycle of the switch
No persistent changes to switch — all RAM-only

target_architecture.go_version_note: Use Go 1.22 — Go 1.23+ has MIPS async preemption hang bug (golang/go#71591)
target_architecture.nan2008_patch: May need to patch ELF e_flags at offset 36 for NaN2008 kernel compatibility

port_isolation.description: DSA-based VLAN port isolation for rogue DHCP prevention during firmware flashing. When flashing a device connected to a switch port, isolating that port into its own VLAN prevents the freshly flashed device's DHCP server from reaching other network devices.

dsa_topology.port_names: lan1 through lan8 (physical ports 1-8, DSA switch port numbers 8-15)
dsa_topology.default_bridge: br-lan — all lan1-lan8 members, untagged, no explicit bridge-vlan sections by default
dsa_topology.source: OpenWrt DTS rtl8380_zyxel_gs1900.dtsi — SWITCH_PORT(8, 1, internal) through SWITCH_PORT(15, 8, internal)

isolate_commands.description: Move a port into isolated VLAN 999. CPU port remains member so the switch can SSH to the device on the isolated port.

port_suffix_meaning.u: untagged egress — packets sent to the wire without VLAN tag
port_suffix_meaning.u*: untagged + primary VLAN (PVID) — untagged egress AND untagged ingress tagged as this VLAN
port_suffix_meaning.t: tagged egress — packets carry VLAN tag on the wire

local_flag.1: CPU port is member of this VLAN → creates br-lan.999 sub-interface → switch can route to isolated device
local_flag.0: CPU port NOT a member → isolated port cannot talk to switch CPU → NO SSH access to target device
local_flag.required: MUST be 1 for conwrt flashing — the switch needs SSH/TFTP access to the target

restore_commands.description: Remove VLAN 999 and return the port to default br-lan membership.
restore_commands.find_vlan_section_index: uci show network | grep 'bridge-vlan.*vlan=999' | cut -d. -f2 | cut -d[ -f2 | cut -d] -f1
restore_commands.note: After deleting the isolation VLAN, the port returns to the default untagged bridge membership. Network restart takes ~10 seconds on this device. All ports experience brief connectivity loss during restart.

gotchas: A port can only be untagged member of ONE bridge VLAN. If an explicit bridge-vlan for VLAN 1 already exists, the target port must be removed from it before adding to VLAN 999.
DEVICE_CLAIM_FAILED error if a port is assigned to multiple bridges — each port can belong to only one bridge device.
Network restart causes ~10 second connectivity loss on ALL ports — plan accordingly if other devices depend on this switch.
After network restart, the switch's br-lan.999 interface gets an IP only if the isolated device provides DHCP — otherwise assign a static IP manually (ip addr add 192.168.1.2/24 dev br-lan.999).
DSA VLAN filtering is enabled by default for Realtek RTL838x in OpenWrt 24.10+/25.x — no extra packages or kernel modules needed.
The known DSA FDB bug (openwrt/openwrt#11650) affects VLAN-tagged + WiFi roaming — irrelevant for pure Ethernet port isolation on a switch with no WiFi.

flow: 1. Isolate target port into VLAN 999 (before PoE power-on)
2. Add secondary IP on br-lan.999 for flash subnet (e.g. 192.168.1.2/24)
3. Enable PoE on target port — device boots in isolated VLAN
4. Flash device via SSH/TFTP within isolated VLAN (DHCP offers cannot escape)
5. Post-flash: configure SSH keys, disable DHCP, set IP
6. Restore port to default VLAN — device joins production network
benefits_over_overlay: Works with ALL flash methods (sysupgrade, recovery-http, TFTP, OEM-HTTP, extreme-rdwr-tftp)
No race condition — port is isolated BEFORE device powers on
Device's DHCP offers never leave the isolated VLAN — broadcast domain is contained
Transparent to the target device — no config changes needed on it
No overlay tarball generation needed — simpler code path
